key_insight: An interpretable deep learning pipeline converts model attention into patient-specific radiomic signatures for tumor classification and biomarker discovery.
clinical_relevance: This could improve non-invasive tumor characterization by making imaging biomarkers more reproducible and easier to interpret for clinical research use.
method: Use a segmentation model to delineate tumors accurately.
Apply Grad-CAM to locate diagnostically important regions as candidate signatures.
Validate extracted signatures with deep learning, radiomics, and SHAP-based feature interpretation.
evidence_signal: The framework was evaluated on BUSI, KiTS, BraTS, and a private UF Health renal CT cohort, where signature-based models outperformed conventional whole-tumor radiomics in discrimination and interpretability.
watch_item: Performance is promising, but the approach still requires careful external validation and workflow standardization before clinical deployment.
paper_title: An Interpretable Deep Learning Framework for Discovery and Clinical Validation of Deep Radiomic Signatures in Tumor Classification
